name: Minced Lamb Samosas
recipeIngredient: 2 Tablespoons Chopped fresh mint or Coriander leaves
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------------------DOUGH-----------------------------------
225 grams Plain flour
1/2 teaspoon Salt
3 Tablespoons Oil
Approximately 100 milliliters Hot water
Oil for deep frying
1. To make the filling, heat the ghee in a saucepan and fry garlic,
ginger, and half the onion until onion is soft. Add curry powder, salt,
vinegar, and mix well. Add minced lamb and fry over high heat, stirring
constantly until meat changes color. Turn down the heat and add hot water.
Cover the pan and cook until the meat is tender and all the liquid has been
absorbed. Stirring frequently towards the end of cooking to prevent the meat
from sticking to the base of the pan. Sprinkle with garam masala and chopped
mint or coriander, remove from heat, and allow to cool. Mix in the reserved
chopped onion.
2. To make the dough, sift together the flour and salt. Rub in the oil.
Add enough water to form a stiff dough. Knead for 10 minutes until smooth.
3. Divide into 12 balls. Roll each ball into a round about 15 centimeters
across (6 inches). Cut in half.
4. Pick up one half, flatten it slightly, and form a cone, sealing the
overlapping edge with a little water. Fill the cone with approximately 1.5
teaspoons of the filling and seal the top with a little water.
5. Make all the samosas in the same way.
6. Heat the oil in a karahi (wok) over medium heat. Put as many samosas as
you can into the hot oil and fry until crisp and golden. Drain on paper
towels. Serve with a chutney or your favorite chili or tomato sauce.
